food_subgroup: Lamb and mutton — rib rack, roasted or grilled

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03311349 represents lamb rib rack food product; used as closest parent as no exact entry for Malaysian-style grilled lamb rack exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers cooked lamb meat broadly; no specific rack/rib cut code available; used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227197003 represents lamb as a food substance; used as closest parent as no specific rack cut code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of lamb rack consists of approximately 250 g (3–4 cutlets), as served at EC Coffee Bar, Carpenter Street, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Lamb, domestic, rib, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 1/4 inch fat, choice, cooked, roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 17082)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for roasted lamb rib (separable lean and fat, trimmed to 1/4 inch fat, choice) used as the closest approved source for a grilled/roasted lamb rack. Values represent cooked lamb rib with typical fat trim. Kindfire preparation (grilled with herb marinade) is consistent with this profile. Minor marinade contribution to sodium is acknowledged but not separately quantified.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure cooked meat contains negligible digestible carbohydrate (0 g per 100 g). The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature consistently assign a GI of effectively 0 to pure meat products with no carbohydrate content. Per the zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule convention, glycemic_index is set to 1 (lowest valid positive sentinel) to satisfy validator requirements; gi_category is low; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~72 mg per 100 g); low carbohydrate; high protein
hypertension.guidance: Lamb rack has a relatively low sodium content per 100 g; individuals with hypertension should monitor total portion size and avoid high-sodium marinades or sauces that may be added during restaurant preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero digestible carbohydrate; high protein; moderate fat
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lamb rack contains no digestible carbohydrate and has a glycemic index of effectively zero, making it a suitable protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderate portions without high-carbohydrate accompaniments.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~8.8 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (~97 mg per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lamb rack contains moderate levels of saturated fat and dietary cholesterol; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 100–150 g and prefer leaner cuts or trimmed preparations to reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lamb rack (also known as rack of lamb) is a premium cut of lamb consisting of the rib section, typically comprising 7–8 rib bones, often French-trimmed for presentation. In the context of this record, it is served as 'Kindfire Lamb Rack' at EC Coffee Bar on Carpenter Street, Singapore, a Malaysian-cuisine restaurant. The dish is typically prepared by marinating the rack in a blend of herbs (rosemary, thyme), garlic, olive oil, salt, and pepper, then grilling or roasting at high heat to achieve a caramelised crust while retaining a pink, tender interior. The flavour profile is rich, savoury, and slightly gamey, with a juicy texture from the intramuscular fat of the rib cut. Nutritionally, lamb rack is a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate food with moderate to high fat content, predominantly from saturated and monounsaturated fatty acids. Per 100 g of cooked lamb rib, it provides approximately 294 kcal, 24.6 g protein, 21 g fat, and 0 g carbohydrate. It is an excellent source of zinc, vitamin B12, iron, and selenium. The absence of carbohydrate means it has no glycemic impact, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate and diabetic dietary patterns. However, its saturated fat and cholesterol content warrant moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease. Lamb is halal by default when slaughtered according to Islamic rites, making it widely consumed in Malaysian and Singaporean Muslim communities. The dish is typically served as a main course at dinner, often accompanied by roasted vegetables, mashed potato, or a red wine jus.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a lamb rack?
Is lamb rack suitable for diabetics?
What is the protein content of lamb rack?
Is lamb rack high in saturated fat?
Can I eat lamb rack on a low-carb diet?
What nutrients are in lamb rack?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Kindfire Lamb Rack or Malaysian-style grilled lamb rack exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy for roasted lamb rib (NDB 17082 / FDC 174352), which is the closest approved source. The preparation method (grilling/roasting with herb marinade) is consistent with the USDA roasted profile. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact restaurant recipe and marinade composition are unknown.
notes: 1. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for cooked lamb rib; this is naturally zero as pure meat contains no digestible carbohydrate. sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as meat contains no dietary fibre. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA source. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is confirmed zero per USDA source for this cut. 2. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this food contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0. 3. trans_fat_g of 0.5 g per 100 g reflects naturally occurring conjugated linoleic acid (CLA) and vaccenic acid present in ruminant fat, as reported by USDA. 4. Sodium value of 72 mg per 100 g reflects the plain roasted cut; actual restaurant sodium may be higher due to marinade and seasoning. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all reported from USDA FDC 174352. 6. The food_name has been normalised to 'Lamb Rack' as the canonical English name; 'Kindfire Lamb Rack' is a branded restaurant name preserved in aliases. 7. Multilingual names follow the Singaporean dish rule (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the dish is served in Singapore by a Malaysian-cuisine restaurant.

Iteration 2: Corrected `glycemic_index` from 0 to 1 as per validator feedback for q4_glycemic_completeness. Verified aliases_by_language consistency — all strings in aliases_by_language confirmed present verbatim in flat aliases. Verified all -1 sentinel values are documented in notes.
